drafts.subject: The dish we keep putting back on the menu
drafts.htmlBody:
THE QUARRY

Some dishes earn a permanent place — this is one of them.

Our pan-seared duck breast comes to the table with a crisp, rendered skin giving way to meat that's rosy and tender at the center. It sits over a celery root purée that's silky without being heavy, finished with a tart cherry reduction and a scattering of toasted hazelnuts for bite. It's the kind of plate that makes the table go quiet for a moment.

Available now through the end of May, while the season holds. We'd recommend pairing it with a glass of our current Willamette Valley Pinot Noir — the kitchen had exactly that in mind when they built the dish.

Reserve Your Table

We'll see you at the table,
The Quarry

The Quarry · 3960 Highway Z, New Melle, MO 63385
thequarrystl.com · Facebook

drafts.subject: This week at The Quarry: Plein Air art, live music & boots
drafts.innerHtml:
This week at The Quarry

April 29 - May 4, 2026

It is a full slate this week - local artists at the lake, live music three nights running, line dancing and bourbon Saturday, and a sunrise yoga session that ends with brunch and a glass of wine.

New Melle "Paint Out" Show & Sale

Thursday, April 30 - 5:00 PM

The Augusta Plein Air painters spend the day painting New Melle, then bring their finished work to The Quarry. Come down to see what they captured, and if a piece moves you, take it home. Judging starts at 5:30 PM, so arrive early.

Boots and Bourbon

Saturday, May 2 - Doors 5:30 PM

DJ kicks off at 6:00 with dance lessons. Social dancing starts at 7:00. Bring your boots.

21+ · $5 cover · No reservations needed

Un-Wine-D & Align - Yoga with Charity

Saturday, May 2 - 9:00-10:00 AM

Stretch, sip, and soak in the vineyard vibes. A relaxing yoga session surrounded by The Quarry, with a glass of wine, mimosa, or bloody mary after class, plus 10% off the brunch menu (included with your ticket). Beginners and seasoned yogis welcome.

To reserve: yogawithcharity@gmail.com

Live Music This Weekend

Friday, May 1 · 7-10 PMJanet Martin
Saturday, May 2 · 1-4 PMDave Caputo
Sunday, May 3 · 1-4 PMMixology

See you at The Quarry.

The Quarry
drafts.preheader: Augusta Plein Air painters Thursday. Boots & Bourbon Saturday. Live music Fri/Sat/Sun.

drafts.subject: {firstName}, your May wines are ready to pick up
drafts.htmlBody:
THE QUARRY
Drinks  ·  Bites  ·  Live Music  ·  Golf

MAY WINE CLUB RELEASE

Your May Selections Are In

{firstName}, your May release is ready and waiting. We've curated two bottles this month that lean into the season — lighter on their feet, longer on the finish.

This Month's Picks

We'll have your full tasting notes ready at pickup, but here's the short version: one white that drinks like a warm afternoon, and a red that's been waiting for a reason to open early. Both are worth lingering over.

When to Pick Up

  • We're open Wednesday through Sunday
  • Pick up at the host stand — we'll have your order ready
  • Stay for a glass and let us walk you through the pairings

As always, your member discount applies to any additional bottles you'd like to take home. Ask your server about what's pouring well right now — the bar team has strong opinions this month.

See you soon,
The Quarry

drafts.subject: The dish we can't stop talking about
drafts.htmlBody:
THE QUARRY
Drinks  ·  Bites  ·  Live Music  ·  Golf

Menu Spotlight

The Kind of Dish That Makes You Slow Down

Some plates arrive and you just know — this one's worth savoring. Our pan-seared duck breast is exactly that: a deep mahogany crust giving way to rose-pink, tender meat, finished with a cherry-port reduction that walks the line between sweet and earthy in all the right ways.

It comes plated over a smooth celery root purée, with a handful of crispy shallots adding just enough texture to keep every bite interesting. It's the kind of entrée that makes a Wednesday night feel like a special occasion.

No reservations needed for dinner — just walk in, find a seat, and let us take it from there.

See you soon,
The Quarry

drafts.subject: This week at The Quarry — May 26–31
drafts.htmlBody:
THE QUARRY
Drinks  ·  Bites  ·  Live Music  ·  Golf

THIS WEEK AT THE QUARRY

A Quiet Week to Simply Come In

Sometimes the best nights out are the unscheduled ones. We don't have special events lined up this week — just good food, a full wine list, and the kind of atmosphere that makes a Wednesday feel like a Friday.

We're open Wednesday through Sunday, no reservations needed for dining. Walk in, find a seat, and let the week slow down a little.

Always On

  • A kitchen turning out dishes worth the drive to New Melle
  • A wine bar stocked with bottles to suit any mood or occasion
  • Hole-In-One Golf for a round before or after dinner
  • Live music most weekends — check back soon as we update the calendar

Planning something bigger? We book private events, weddings, and corporate gatherings. Learn more about private events.

See you soon,
The Quarry

drafts.subject: The dish worth the drive to New Melle
drafts.htmlBody:
THE QUARRY
Drinks  ·  Bites  ·  Live Music  ·  Golf

Menu Spotlight

Some plates just stop the conversation.

There's a moment when something lands on the table and everyone at the table goes quiet — not because they're being polite, but because they're already reaching for a fork. That's what our kitchen is after every single night.

This week, we're putting a spotlight on one of the dishes our regulars keep coming back for: a beautifully seared duck breast, rested to a perfect blush, served over a silky celery root purée with a tart cherry reduction and a scattering of toasted hazelnuts. Rich without being heavy. Elegant without being fussy.

It pairs remarkably well with a glass of Pinot Noir from our rotating by-the-glass list — ask your server what's pouring this week and let them find you the right match.

No reservations needed for dining — just walk in Wednesday through Sunday and find your table. We'll handle the rest.

See you soon,
The Quarry

Drinks  ·  Bites  ·  Live Music  ·  Golf

THIS WEEK AT THE QUARRY

Your week, June 2–7

We're open Wednesday through Sunday and ready for you — but it looks like we're keeping this week's calendar close to the chest. Check back soon for live music announcements and upcoming events.

In the meantime, the kitchen is firing, the wine list is deep, and the Hole-In-One Golf bays are open. No reservation needed — just walk in and settle in.

Have a group of eight or more? A birthday, a work outing, a reason to gather? We'd love to host you. Ask about private events and large-party bookings.

See you soon,
The Quarry

drafts.subject: Your June wine selections are ready, {firstName}
drafts.htmlBody:
THE QUARRY
Drinks  ·  Bites  ·  Live Music  ·  Golf

June Wine Club Release

Your June Bottles Are In

Each month we taste through more wines than we'd care to admit so you don't have to. June's release leans into the season — bright, food-friendly whites and a red that punches well above its price.

This Month's Selections

We're not publishing the full tasting notes here — those are tucked inside your release package along with pairing suggestions from our kitchen. But here's what to expect when you pick up this month:

  • A crisp, mineral-driven white that belongs on the patio with anything off the grill
  • A lush, aromatic rosé built for long summer evenings
  • A structured red with enough backbone to age, but plenty of reason to open it now

Pick Up Your Release

Your June selections are available for pickup at The Quarry Wednesday through Sunday. If you'd like to enjoy them on-site, our team is happy to open a bottle at the table — member pricing applies.

Have questions about your membership or this month's wines? Reach out at info@thequarrynewmelle.com and we'll get back to you.

See you soon,
The Quarry

drafts.subject: The dish worth the drive to New Melle
drafts.htmlBody:
THE QUARRY
Drinks  ·  Bites  ·  Live Music  ·  Golf

Menu Spotlight

Some plates just stay with you.

There's a reason our wood-fired half chicken keeps showing up in your conversations after dinner. The skin comes off the fire lacquered and crackling, the meat underneath still holding every bit of its juice — and the whole thing lands on a bed of roasted garlic whipped potatoes that you'll be thinking about on the drive home.

It's finished with a thyme-scented pan jus and a scattering of crispy shallots — the kind of detail that tells you someone in that kitchen actually cares. Simple on the surface. Anything but underneath.

We're open Wednesday through Sunday, no reservations needed for dining. Walk in, find a seat, and let the kitchen do the rest.

See you soon,
The Quarry

drafts.subject: This week at The Quarry — June 9–13
drafts.htmlBody:
THE QUARRY
Drinks  ·  Bites  ·  Live Music  ·  Golf

This Week at The Quarry

We're pulling together the week's lineup for you — but it looks like our calendar is still catching up. No events have been added for the week of June 9 just yet.

That said, we're open Wednesday through Sunday, and the kitchen, the wine bar, and the golf bays are ready for you. Walk-ins are always welcome — no reservation needed for a table.

Check back soon, or keep an eye on your inbox. When live music and special events are confirmed, you'll hear from us first.

Planning something bigger? Private events, corporate gatherings, and parties of all sizes are something we do well. Tell us about your event and we'll take it from there.

See you soon,
The Quarry

drafts.subject: Music Bingo is filling up — May 22
drafts.htmlBody:
THE QUARRY
Drinks  ·  Bites  ·  Live Music  ·  Golf

Two Weeks Out

70s · 80s · 90s Music Bingo

Our Music Bingo nights have been selling out, and this one is no different. With 21 seats still available, now is the time to lock in your table before they're gone.

Here's how the night works: your table of four gets a beer bucket or a bottle of wine included, then you race to match classic tracks from three decades of hits. Prizes go out all night long — and yes, the competition gets real.

  • Friday, May 22 · 5:30 PM
  • Tables of 4 · $80 per table
  • Beer bucket or bottle of wine included
  • Prizes throughout the night
  • 21 seats remaining

This is a ticketed event, and capacity is capped at 34 guests total — so it stays intimate, which is exactly why it fills up fast. Grab your table while you can.

Reserve your spot

See you on the 22nd,
The Quarry

drafts.subject: The dish everyone keeps asking about
drafts.htmlBody:
THE QUARRY
Drinks  ·  Bites  ·  Live Music  ·  Golf

Worth every bite.

Some dishes earn a permanent place on the menu. Our Quarry Burger is one of them — a half-pound of house-ground beef, smoked gouda, crispy shallots, and a whiskey-bacon jam that does most of the talking.

It comes stacked on a brioche bun with a side of hand-cut fries seasoned with our kitchen's own spice blend. Simple on paper. Anything but in practice.

Pair it with a cold draft or let our bar team suggest something from the wine list — there's a bold red that holds its own against the smoky, savory weight of that jam.

drafts.subject: The dish everyone keeps asking about
drafts.htmlBody:
THE QUARRY
Drinks  ·  Bites  ·  Live Music  ·  Golf

Some things are worth coming back for.

There's a reason our kitchen keeps this one on the menu. The seared salmon — golden-crusted, just yielding at the center — arrives over a bed of roasted garlic whipped potatoes with a lemon-caper butter that pulls the whole plate together in a way that's hard to stop thinking about afterward.

It's the kind of dish that looks effortless and tastes like someone actually cared. Because they did.

Pair it with something from our wine list — our team can point you toward a crisp white or a light, chilled rosé that won't compete — and you've got a weeknight dinner that feels like a proper occasion.

This Weekend at The Quarry

DJ Earl at The Quarry

SUN · MAY 24

drafts.subject: The dish we can't take off the menu
drafts.htmlBody:
THE QUARRY
Drinks  ·  Bites  ·  Live Music  ·  Golf

Menu Spotlight

Some things earn a permanent place at the table.

Our pan-seared salmon has become exactly that — a fillet with crisp, golden skin giving way to a tender, flaky center, finished with a lemon-caper beurre blanc that's bright without being sharp. It arrives over a bed of roasted fingerling potatoes and wilted spinach, and it looks as considered as it tastes.

It's the kind of dish that starts as a "I'll try something different" order and ends as the reason you come back. Quietly confident. No garnish for garnish's sake.

We're open Wednesday through Sunday, walk-ins welcome. If you're planning something larger — a private dinner, a celebration — we'd love to talk.

See you soon,
The Quarry

drafts.subject: The dish worth the drive to New Melle
drafts.htmlBody:
THE QUARRY
Drinks  ·  Bites  ·  Live Music  ·  Golf

Menu Spotlight

Some things are worth savoring slowly.

There's a reason certain dishes become the reason people come back. This week, we're turning the spotlight on one of the kitchen's quiet standouts — a plate that earns its place on the menu every single time it goes out.

We're talking about our Pan-Seared Duck Breast: a deeply golden crust giving way to tender, rosy meat, finished with a cherry-port reduction that's rich without being heavy. It arrives alongside whipped parsnip and a scattering of wilted greens — the kind of plate that makes the table go quiet for a moment.

It's the sort of dish that rewards a slower evening — a good glass of wine, a comfortable seat, no rush. That's exactly what we're here for.

Join us Wednesday through Sunday. No reservations needed for dining — just walk in and let us take care of the rest.

See you soon,
The Quarry

drafts.subject: The dish we can't stop ordering this summer
drafts.htmlBody:
THE QUARRY
Drinks  ·  Bites  ·  Live Music  ·  Golf

Menu Spotlight

The Seared Salmon That Earns Its Place

Some dishes just belong to a season. Our pan-seared salmon — golden-crusted, finished with a bright lemon-caper beurre blanc and served over herbed farro with shaved fennel — is the one we keep coming back to all summer long.

The farro gives it enough weight to feel like a real meal. The fennel keeps it light. The sauce ties everything together with just enough acid to cut through the richness of the fish.

It's the kind of plate that makes you slow down a little — which, honestly, is the whole point of a Wednesday night at The Quarry.

We're open Wednesday through Sunday. Walk in, grab a seat at the bar or on the patio, and let us take it from there.

See you soon,
The Quarry
